description: Move Analytics Shiny App into its own repository and stop depending on the AutoPlots source tree.
notes: Keep AutoPlots as an external package dependency.

description: Keep all R source files directly under R/ with no nested R/services, R/registries, R/project, or R/utils directories.
Move page-level UI/server logic into flat page module files.
notes: Preserve stable module IDs and shared state ownership.

description: Add app-owned UI helper functions for cards, sections, empty states, status badges, actions, controls, previews, and code panels.
Introduce CSS variables and theme classes for future theme switching.
notes: Keep inputs readable in all themes.

description: Represent saved plots as report artifacts through adapter helpers while preserving saved_plots behavior.
Allow manual text blocks to be created, previewed, organized, and included in mixed reports.
Create internal table rendering, themes, and export framework using reactable where needed.
Allow users to create and manage table artifacts.
Create generic aq_artifact structure for plots, tables, text, metrics, forecasts, and narratives.
Create central UI for viewing, previewing, editing metadata, hiding/showing, exporting, and removing artifacts.
notes: Must remain separate from report plans.

description: Render plots, text, and tables together in grid and section report previews.
notes: Leave AutoPlots plot-only display functions intact.

description: Persist configs, artifacts, layout settings, export settings, and project metadata.
notes: Do not persist htmlwidgets as primary project state.

description: Save/load portable bundles containing project state, data, and exports.
Support report HTML export, generated R code export, and combined export actions.
notes: Generated code must use AutoPlots high-level functions.

description: Export table artifact data to CSV and XLSX.
Define lightweight registry for planned analysis modules and module runner helpers.
notes: No real modules required for registry skeleton.

description: Wire AutoQuant EDA outputs into artifacts through an adapter.
Create report plan structures for curated artifact ordering and layout recommendations.
Generate a recommended EDA report plan from AutoQuant EDA artifacts.
Allow users to preview, duplicate, rename, edit, archive, and apply report plans.
notes: Plan edits must not delete artifacts.

description: Document Electron wrapper smoke testing and repo ownership boundaries.
notes: Electron wrapper is a separate repo.

description: Define service_result pattern and service boundary expectations.
Document future GenAI architecture, policies, proposals, and action gates.
Document UI framework rules, helper layer, extension model, and anti-patterns.
Document generator/display separation and artifact library responsibilities.
Define analysis module contracts, registry direction, AutoQuant mapping, and anti-patterns.
Document internal table rendering, theming, and export framework.
Document report plan model, validation, preview/apply/edit lifecycle, and Artifact Library separation.

feature: Stabilize AutoQuant EDA adapter/report plan workflow
description: Harden EDA artifact normalization, recommended plan generation, plan preview, apply behavior, and project persistence.
notes: Current top priority before adding more modules.

description: Map all meaningful AutoQuant EDA outputs into stable report sections.
Adapt AutoQuant model readiness outputs into artifacts.
notes: Add only after EDA plan workflow stabilizes.

description: Create recommended plan for model readiness artifacts.
Document local trusted code execution model, permissions, logs, outputs, and artifact conversion.
Create code run request/result/tracker models.

feature: Manual local trusted code runner prototype
description: Run explicitly approved local code and capture results without GenAI automation.
Convert code outputs into table, text, plot, or metric artifacts.
Show previous code runs, status, logs, and produced artifacts.
notes: Keep separate from Artifact Library inventory.

description: Improve mixed report preview spacing, section hierarchy, artifact wrappers, and empty states.
Improve library scanning, filters, preview behavior, metadata editing, and export ergonomics.

feature: Project save/load QA for artifacts/plans/code runs
description: Add manual and helper QA for persistence of artifacts, plans, and future code run history.

feature: Electron smoke test after module additions
description: Run Electron wrapper smoke tests after analysis module and report-plan changes.
notes: Fix Electron-only issues in wrapper repo.

description: Adapt target analysis outputs into report artifacts.
notes: Wait until EDA and model readiness patterns are stable.

description: Adapt model insight outputs into artifacts.
notes: Likely depends on model object conventions.

description: Design SHAP artifact structure and module contract.
Implement SHAP analysis adapter/module.
Build narrow CatBoost training/scoring artifact-generator workflow for regression and binary classification.
notes: V1 should return training/scoring artifacts and downstream handoff metadata; not a broad modeling workbench.

description: Generate forecast plots, tables, metrics, and narrative artifacts.

feature: Recommended report plans for each module
description: Create editable recommended plans for each analysis module.
Create concise data profile object for recommendations and narratives.
Add settings surface for provider/model keys and future policy controls.
Define allowed GenAI actions and their required permissions.
Create policy object for allowed data access, actions, and mutation rules.
notes: Required before GenAI can change state.

description: Represent GenAI suggestions as reviewable proposals.
Add hooks that approve or reject proposed GenAI actions.
notes: Must cover code and project mutations.

description: Suggest plot types and mappings from data profile.
Generate chart titles, captions, and artifact labels.
Generate report section summary text artifacts.
Review report plans and artifacts for gaps or risks.
Propose a starter artifact selection and report plan.
Allow GenAI to propose code runs through approval workflow.
Let GenAI perform multi-step app actions under strict policy.
notes: Do not start until policy/action scaffolding is mature.

description: Define what local code may do and what requires permission.
Add permission hooks for running code and converting outputs.
Create structured request/result objects for code execution.
Track code, inputs, status, outputs, and produced artifacts.
Capture stdout, warnings, errors, and diagnostics for each run.
Allow rerunning a stored code run with the same or updated data.
Export code run history and generated report code as a reproducible script.
notes: Avoid mixing with AutoPlots code export until stable.

description: Route GenAI code proposals through code runner approval.
Improve Plot Builder ergonomics without changing registry-driven behavior.
Add alternate library views for scanning artifacts.
Improve preview readability with section counts, badges, missing refs, and warnings.
Make report previews feel presentation-ready.
Improve empty states across Data, Plot Builder, Artifact Library, Layouts, Export, and Project pages.
Create page for app settings and future GenAI/code runner settings.
Expose light/dark/pimp theme selection in the UI.
Save selected theme in project/app settings.
Add carousel display for report artifacts.
Add drag/drop canvas display mode.
Allow resizable report artifact cards.
Define app-wide permission model for GenAI, code runner, export, and mutation actions.
Support future role/permission distinctions.
Persist app-level settings outside project state where appropriate.
Create general audit log for important app actions.
Track GenAI proposals, approvals, denials, and actions.
Track code runner requests, approvals, execution, and outputs.
Review local execution, file access, export, Electron wrapper, and GenAI policy boundaries.
Document and automate wrapper packaging flow.
Document local setup, dependency installation, Electron build, and release packaging.
notes: Keep app vs wrapper ownership clear.

description: Provide useful sample datasets for smoke testing and demos.
Add repeatable smoke scripts for app launch and core workflows.
Keep README aligned with app structure, dependencies, AutoPlots dependency, and local development workflow.
Keep startup dependency checks friendly and accurate.
Create checklist for browser Shiny, Electron, export, bundles, dependencies, and known limitations.
Run Electron wrapper smoke test before release.
Document unsupported modules, export limitations, GenAI/code runner status, and Electron caveats.
Add problem-type-specific SHAP contracts, registry entries, adapter shells, report-plan specs, and QA helpers without implementing SHAP computation.
notes: Regression and binary SHAP are scaffolded separately; multiclass and three-way interactions remain deferred.

description: Wire AutoQuant regression SHAP artifacts into the app artifact/report-plan workflow using precomputed Shap_ columns.
notes: AnalyticsShinyApp validates inputs and normalizes AutoQuant plot/table/text artifacts; it does not compute SHAP values.

description: Wire AutoQuant binary classification SHAP artifacts into the app artifact/report-plan workflow using precomputed Shap_ columns.
notes: AnalyticsShinyApp validates positive class, prediction scale, threshold context, and normalizes AutoQuant plot/table/text artifacts; it does not compute SHAP values.

description: Document CatBoost Builder v1 scope, AutoQuant/app boundaries, scored output contract, downstream handoff, persistence, QA, and phases.
Define generate_catboost_builder_artifacts() contract, fixtures, output metadata, scored data contract, and QA helpers.
notes: AutoQuant owns training/scoring and returns artifacts, scored output, and downstream handoff metadata; support regression and binary only.

feature: Analytics Shiny App CatBoost Builder adapter
description: Add app adapter after AutoQuant generator contract stabilizes; normalize artifacts and report plans.
notes: App validates config, calls only AutoQuant::generate_catboost_builder_artifacts(), preserves scored data metadata, and creates report plans.

description: Expose user-triggered actions from CatBoost Builder scored output to Model Assessment, problem-specific Model Insights, and problem-specific SHAP when valid.
notes: No automatic downstream execution; handoff uses scored_data and recommended configs through run_analysis_module().

feature: Custom code hooks for workflow stages
description: Create a lightweight hook contract so workflow stages can queue pre-stage, post-stage, or standalone exploratory code through the existing Code Runner without auto-running or bypassing local_trusted controls.
notes: Hooks create Code Runner draft requests with workflow-stage metadata; execution and output-to-artifact conversion remain user-triggered.
